In 2502, we introduce the "MainWorkCenterPlant" field in the Maintenance Task List API. Previously, there was a discrepancy between the Order APIs and Task List API: the Order APIs used the "MainWorkCenterPlant" field, while the Task List API used "Plant". By standardizing the field name across both APIs, we ensure a more coherent and reliable development environment.

The exposure of "MainWorkCenterPlant" in both the task list header and operations API promises a unified approach to work center plant data management.

Image 3: Main Work Center Plant field in Task list API

The existing "Plant" field remains available for now in the Task List API; however, it is slated for deprecation in future releases. SAP strongly recommends users transition to the "MainWorkCenterPlant" field to avoid potential disruptions when the old field is phased out.

This transition period lets developers adjust their implementations gradually, ensuring applications continue to function seamlessly as SAP updates its offerings.

Item Category BadI in Maintenance Order

The new item category BAdI in maintenance orders, EAM_ITEM_CAT_DET_CNTRL, enables the automatic proposal of item categories in maintenance orders, utilizing all visible fields available in the component and service screens as potential input parameters.

Utilizing the EAM_ITEM_CAT_DET_CNTRL BAdI, it is now possible to customize item category determination directly within the materials tab of the Maintenance Order. Through custom logic, users can fine-tune item category proposals, enabling more accurate and context-sensitive defaults.

Image 4: Item category BadI in Maintenance Order

SAP Asset Performance Management 

SAP Asset Performance Management empowers asset owners, plant managers, and reliability engineers to optimize maintenance strategies, measure and improve asset performance, and follow through on your sustainability objectives.

Stay tuned for the blog post from Kate Dowle where she will share the latest updates on APM.

 

More Information / Additional Links 

You will find a collection of SAP S/4HANA Cloud product centric blog posts here:

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Asset Management – The Link Collection
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Manufacturing & Supply Chain - The Link Collection
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Supply Chain - The Link Collection
  • Discrete Industries in SAP S/4HANA Cloud - The Link Collection

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. 

Follow me on LinkedIn and stay up-to-date to our latest enablement assets: Fernanda Rodrigues | LinkedIn

 

📽️ Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!   

Between January 21 and January 24, we conducted a series of 21 engaging live sessions to showcase the thrilling innovations included in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. If you missed the live sessions, don't worry! You can still access the whole series or select the sessions that interest you the most. Seize this opportunity to watch them on demand at your convenience–anywhere, anytime.  

🔎Find All of Our Assets   

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: SAP User Experience Q1/2025 Update – Part 1: Many New Innovations Available (AI, Joule and More)
entry.content:
This series of posts gives an overview of user experience innovations in the cloud from the last six months, covering our whole portfolio, in particular SAP SuccessFactors and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502. It also gives an overview of innovations in our UI technologies, making it ever easier to build and integrate UIs.

AI is revolutionizing user experience, giving users capabilities unheard of just a few years ago. SAP is harnessing the power of AI to help business users work more effectively: Joule is your copilot for seamless access to a variety of products a user needs, now covering 80% of the most-used transactions. Generative AI embedded in business applications gives users unprecedented insights and convenience. Beyond that, we continue to provide new applications with a great user experience, as well as UX-enhancing features.

How is AI, and especially generative AI, changing user experience for business users? Essentially via one or more of these patterns:

  • Summarizing business data in an easy to understand way – in text or graphical form.
  • Guiding and Recommending options or actions based on the current business context.
  • Creating proposals for texts or business data and entities such as assignments, performance goals with milestones etc., often based on natural language input.
  • Helping you write texts to people internally and externally, with our AI writing assistant.
  • Interacting via natural language with your application, e.g. search, filtering, analytical queries – as an alternative to established UI interactions.
  • Assisting you with all of the above, wherever you are, for all our cloud products – via Joule, your copilot for SAP systems.

Our overarching goal is to provide a great user experience everywhere. AI is a powerful means for achieving that, but of course you can also have a great user experience in applications without embedded AI.

A good user experience also needs to address teamwork, not just the experience of each individual – in other words, helping SAP users collaborate and work together.

I’ve collected examples from our latest releases to show how we are doing this – starting with our cross-product UX innovations in Joule and central entry points before looking at product UX innovations. Here is what the series will cover; as each part becomes available in the next couple of weeks I will update this list with links to each post:

  1. Central UX Services (this post)
    • Joule – latest UX innovations
    • Central entry points and services:
      • SAP Start / SAP Build Work Zone
      • SAP Task Center
      • SAP Mobile Start
  1. ERP: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 and SAP Fiori launchpad (AI, Joule and More).
  2. HR: SAP SuccessFactors (AI, Joule and More).
  3. Spend: SAP Ariba and SAP Fieldglass.
  4. Selling: SAP Customer Experience and SAP Industry Cloud – Retail.
  5. Platform: SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Business Technology Platform.
  6. UI Design and Technology – web and mobile.

Before we get started: if you haven’t already seen them, do have a look at the overview of public cloud UX innovations I posted in February and July 2024:

  • SAP UX Innovations in Public Cloud Products, Joule, AI, Entry Points, Apple Vision Pro and more.
  • SAP UX Latest 2024 Innovations in Public Cloud: Joule, GenAI and More.

This series of posts includes Joule and AI UX highlights from the above products, but for a more comprehensive overview of our recent AI innovations and SAP’s Business AI strategy, with many UX examples, have a look at:

  • SAP Business AI: Release Highlights Q4 2024
  • White Paper: SAP Business AI.

Joule User Experience Innovations

Joule is the AI-powered copilot for SAP systems, offering a powerful new user experience, using natural language to access our cloud products and providing a seamless flow for the user.

Now, Joule is generally available for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502, as well as for our central entry points in the web SAP Start and SAP Build Work Zone (both standard and advanced editions). You can find examples in the respective product sections in this series; this section focusses on generic capabilities recently introduced or coming soon for Joule. If you want an overview of what’s new, or want to set up Joule in your landscape, have a look at the documentation:

  • What’s New for Joule.
  • Integrating Joule with SAP Solutions.

Joule already covers 80% of the most-used transactions in SAP systems and 1,200+ skills, tailored to business roles across SAP’s cloud portfolio such as hiring manager, billing specialist, procurement manager etc.

Joule Available on Mobile

Joule is also now generally available for mobile, running natively with SAP Mobile Start. Figure 1 shows examples of Joule running on a mobile phone.

Figure 1: Examples showing Joule integrated in SAP Mobile Start, showing from left to right: the start page, the informational pattern accessing SAP Help Portal, and the transactional pattern used to interact with SAP S/4HANA Cloud and then with SAP SuccessFactors. ALT Text: Four images are shown: the start page shows a “How can I help you” text, followed by some suggested prompts as buttons; the informational pattern shows the prompt “How can I customize a sales order”. Below that, the sixteen-line written answer is shown, followed by a “Search results” selector and three top search results. The navigational pattern shows the prompt “Show sales orders with delivery status not completed”, below that, the reply from Joule “Here’s what I’ve found” is followed by a card containing three lines and an “Open” button. A second transactional example with the prompt “Show profile of Maria Alvares”, followed by the result and a button for navigating to her profile.

On Apple devices you can now use Siri and Siri Shortcuts to seamlessly interact with Joule - which works even better with Apple Intelligence. Simply say "Hey Siri, ask SAP. Show my sales orders with delivery status not completed" and Joule will provide you with the respective content within the app.

Note that as of today, not all your prompts and web apps are supported on the phone; however, we are continuously adding further Joule business skills and scenarios.

Find out more here:

  • Joule on Mobile & Start Screen Personalization: SAP Mobile Start V2.1 - Release Update

Joule Analytical Pattern coming soon

In Q1/2025 we plan to provide a controlled release of the new analytical pattern for Joule, for those products listed above for which Joule is available. General availability currently planned for Q2/2025 (as usual for forward-looking statements, our plans are subject to change). It is powered by SAP Analytics Cloud, with data from models that are indexed by the Just Ask feature of SAP Analytics Cloud.

This means that Joule will soon support four interaction patterns, as shown in Figure 2.

  1. Informational pattern, for querying written documents, such as SAP documentation in the SAP Help Portal, or customer policies etc.
  2. Navigational pattern, for finding and navigating to applications.
  3. Transactional pattern, for interacting directly with business applicatinos
  4. Planned New: Analytical pattern, for answering analytical queries directly in Joule.

Figure 2: Joule Interaction Patterns. ALT text: four interaction patterns are shown: informational, with the prompt “what are the key rules for a team outing” followed by an eight-line answer and a link to a source document; navigational, showing a button “Go to Org Chart” after approving a position; transactional, with the text “Below are the new position details set to be hired starting today”, followed by a card summarizing the key details, and a prompt by the user “Send for approval”; Analytical, showing the prompt “What is the hiring versus attrition trend for this job role in the past 12 months?” and the answer shown as a chart with one line for hired and one for percentage attrition, plus numbers showing 124 similar roles hired and 6 role attrition.

Find out more about Joule for Analytics in this blog post:

  • Analytical Insights in Joule (powered by SAP Analytics Cloud), Controlled release in Q1 2025.

Outlook

We are working on making Joule available for further cloud products, as well as making it even more useful via agentic-AI, where Joule coordinates multi-agent systems. An AI agent can autonomously plan, reason, access external tools, work with other agents, and iteratively reflect on progress until it achieves its objective.

Joule will help SAP’s cloud users work faster, get instant insights, and achieve better outcomes by simply asking questions or telling the system what to do. Not only in English, but in their own language. We plan to allow users to interact with Joule in a growing number of languages including German, Spanish, French, and Portuguese. To get an idea of what is planned and what it could look like, accessing multiple products, using English and Spanish (with subtitles), have a look at this:

  • Video: AI is the new UI (3:00).

For more details of what is planned, have a look at the road map for Joule in the SAP Road Map Explorer:

  • Joule Road Map.

Central entry points and services

SAP Start

The integration with Joule in SAP Start is now generally available. The purpose of SAP Start is to bring together relevant business information from SAP for each user in one central place, and also to provide the one place to go to start your SAP applications in the cloud. SAP Start:

  • Gives you quick access to all your applications via the simple yet powerful search field;
  • New: Gives you direct access to your product home pages, as shown in Figure 3.
  • Proactively informs you about your to-dos from all products which are connected to the SAP Task Center;
  • Gives you key insights via insights cards from products connected to SAP Start,
  • Assists you with Joule, your copilot for working with SAP.

Figure 3: SAP Start, now with direct access to product home pages. This example shows direct access to three product home pages in the “Products” section: SAP S/4HANA Cloud, SAP Fieldglass and SAP SuccessFactors. ALT Text: Below the “Products” section the screen shows the To-Dos section, with four cards for pending tasks, the first three with Approve and Reject buttons on them. Below that, the Insights section is shown, with two rows of four cards, starting with lists of My Statements of Work, My Workers, My Team, List of Top 4 Customer Projects.

Now, SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Integrated Business Planning support SAP Start, in addition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, SAP SuccessFactors and SAP Fieldglass. Other cloud products can be integrated to provide tasks via SAP Task Center. You can find out how to set up and integrate cloud products to SAP Start here:

  • Documentation: SAP Start.

SAP Build Work Zone

Joule is now also generally available for SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition:

  • Joule is Now Integrated into SAP Build Work Zone!

SAP Build Work Zone now allows you to integrate web UIs based on HTML5 from other SAP BTP subaccounts. This is done by creating an HTML5 business solution that acts as a content provider in the subaccount.

  • This business solution can be accessed not only by the local subaccount but also by remote subaccounts through destinations.
  • To set up an HTML5 business solution as a content provider, the provider subaccount needs to adjust the HTML5 code to include a cdm.json file, which defines the design-time business content such as roles, groups, spaces, and pages, along with destinations. Find out more here:
    • Simplifying HTML5 Content Consumption Across Subaccounts.

In my blog post from February 2024, I explained that SAP Build Work Zone allows customers to define guided experiences, i.e. wizards, to guide users step-by-step through complex processes.

A major enhancement in the October 2024 release is the ability to model guided processes directly in SAP Build Process Automation, bringing powerful process automation to your SAP Build projects. Get an overview here:

  • Guided Experiences powered by SAP Build.

Just recently, a new wizard setting was added to allow the execution of the same wizard multiple times. This setting is available both in freestyle wizards and in wizards that are based on a Guided Process from SAP Build Process Automation. For example, an admin can enable this option on the Purchase Order wizard, and this will allow users to run this wizard multiple times to purchase different items.

Enhanced Page Builder Experience across SAP Build Work Zone:

The SAP Build Work Zone page builder experience has been significantly enhanced with new features. Users can now enjoy a widget context menu, the ability to move sections up and down, and a section configuration menu, as shown in Figure 4. Additionally, the option to show or hide section headers and various accessibility improvements make the editor more user-friendly and versatile.

Figure 4: SAP Build Work Zone enhanced page builder experience. ALT Text: the image shows a section of the Work Zone screen with a visual editor. The section is partitioned into three sub-sections, each with a button “Add Widget”, the rightmost one shows an open context menu for the button, which shows that you can also add a column. The leftmost section contains a simple list of items, the middle one a bar chart for Project Cloud Revenue, the rightmost a donut chart for Open SAP Incidents.

Here is the guide with recommendations and further details:

  • How to Build and Design a Workpage.

SAP Task Center

Users can conveniently access tasks from many different systems in one place – not only from SAP products, we also provide APIs for integrating tasks from 3rd-party products.

Even more conveniently, users can now be notified of new tasks via e-mail. Also, end user notifications can now be enabled if you are using SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition as central point of entry for accessing your business applications.

SAP Build Work Zone itself can also be set up as a task provider for SAP Task Center. This means that tasks created by SAP Build Work Zone will also appear in SAP Task Center.

Furthermore, SAP Task Center now allows users to forward approvals.

New Intelligent Recommendations Feature Available for SAP Build Process Automation Tasks

We are introducing AI to expedite task processing: intelligent recommendations leverage artificial intelligence to streamline and accelerate task approval processes. They assist users in making decisions by analyzing key attributes relevant to specific business cases and generating a confidence level in percent for their tasks. Figure 5 shows some approvals with a confidence level of 100%, and some with 97.5%.

Figure 5: New Intelligent Recommendations feature in SAP Task Center. ALT Text: An image of the task center list of tasks, with a filter bar at the top with seven fields available, and the field “Task Type” set to “Manager Approval”. The list shows six entries. The new column “Confidence Level” is highlighted, and shows 100% for the first four entries, and 97.5% for the last two.

Find out more here:

  • Enhance Task Approvals with the Power of AI.

SAP Mobile Start

Beyond providing Joule natively for mobile devices, SAP Mobile Start has recently introduced a number of user experience improvements with their 2.0 and 2.1 releases. Here, I would like to highlight the support for ‘new site experience’, giving users a more structured view of applications and information.

Figure 6: SAP Mobile Start with Spaces & Pages from new site experience. ALT Text: Two images of mobile phones. On the left the Apps page, which contains “My Apps Space” showing a list of three page names “Customer Management Page”, “Project management page” and “App Page”. On the right, the App Page is shown, with a list of six apps as cards, with an icon on the left of the app name. The first three (My Inbox, Monitor Situations, Manage My Timesheet) have coloured KPI values displayed on the right.

A further significant enhancement is the support of declarative UI integration cards, which are displayed embedded in the respective pages on the Apps screen.

Figure 7: SAP Mobile Start supports UI integration cards from SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition. ALT Text: Two mobile phone images. On the left Project Apps, which shows a flat card for Project Factsheet and below that a UI integration card showing overall status for active projects, with a list of five projects along with their overall status – four on track, one with “high risk” in red; at the bottom of the card a “See all” button. On the right two further UI integration cards are shown: a list of top 4 projects, and “Cost to Date – Timeline” containing a KPI number for the actual cost to date and below that a line chart of actual and planned cost per month.

For a good and satisfying user experience, being able to personalize the UI so that it fits with how you like to work is a key element. To this end, SAP Mobile Start has now introduced personalization of the start page, allowing you to rearrange the order of sections as well as hiding sections which you don’t need – take a look at it in Figure 8.

Figure 8: SAP Mobile Start personalization of the start screen. ALT Text: Four mobile phone images. On the left the start page, with the sections Insights and News visible. Next, an image of the edit screen, with five sections listed: Latest To-Dos, Favorites, Insights, App Suggestions (deselected), News. Next, an image of the five sections, all selected, now with Favorites first and Latest To-Dos second. On the right, the start page with the sections Favorites at the top (containing flat cards for apps), below that Latest To-Dos (containing larger cards for ToDos, one shocing a purchase requisition with Total Amount $799.99).

To find out more about recent innovations as well as what is planned in our road map, have a look at:

  • SAP Mobile Start V2.0 - Release Update
  • Joule on Mobile & Start Screen Personalization: SAP Mobile Start V2.1 - Release Update
  • SAP Mobile Start Road Map.

Continued soon…

I hope you enjoyed this first part, covering cross-product UX services. I will post part two in the next few days, covering a a large number of UX innovations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 and SAP Fiori launchpad (AI, Joule and More).

entry.title: Product Compliance in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
With this blog, Ralf and I will walk you through below selected key highlights for Product Compliance in SAP S/4Hana Cloud Public Edition 2502:

  • Regulatory Content Updates for Product Marketability, Dangerous Goods Management, and Safety Data Sheets
  • Mixed Loading Check Rules for CFR 49
  • Mixed Loading Check Integration in Sales
  • Extension for Dangerous Goods Packing Instructions
  • Classify Partially Packaged Dangerous Goods

In case you do not need all the information below and briefly want to get an overview of the release highlights, please watch this video with the top three key innovations:

Video#1: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 for Product Compliance: Top 3 key innovations.

Regulatory Content Updates

Business Background

In the area of product compliance, the importance of content is crucial. Ensuring that products meet their target markets' strict legal and regulatory standards is essential, covering areas such as safety, environmental impact, and labeling guidelines. As businesses navigate the complex world of compliance, having access to comprehensive content becomes vital.

In this blog post, we will explore the significant role of content in product compliance processes, highlighting how it supports compliance with regulations and facilitates smooth operations. We will specifically focus on SAP's provision of essential content for various compliance elements, including compliance requirements, graphics, regulatory phrases with translations, listed substances, substance groups, and substance conditions. Join us as we uncover the significance of content in driving excellence in product compliance and aligning with regulations.

Key Feature

With this new 2502 release, SAP delivers new and updated compliance requirements for Product Marketability, Safety Data Sheet Management, and Dangerous Goods Management, which you can use in your product compliance processes.


Figure#1: Stay compliant with your product portfolio using the most recent regulatory updates

Regulatory Content for Product Marketability

In Product Marketability and Chemical Compliance, a compliance requirement contains data for a specific regulation, and this data is delivered as regulatory content for product marketability. In total, 274 compliance requirements are delivered for product marketability, with 150 including regulatory content.
With this feature, new compliance requirement versions have been released:

  • AU AIIC Status (Jul 2024)
  • CA Drug Precursors (Jun 2024)
  • CA DSL+NDSL Status (Jun 2024)
  • CH Chemical Risk Reduction Ordinance Status (Jun 2024)
  • CN IECSC Status (May 2024)
  • CN RoHS (Jun 2024)
  • EU Drug Precursors (May 2024)
  • EU ODS Status (Feb 2024)
  • EU REACH Annex XVII Status (May 2024)
  • EU REACH SVHC Status (Jun 2024)
  • JP CSCL Class 1 and 2 Status (Jul 2024)
  • JP CSCL Exempt Status (Jun 2024)
  • JP CSCL General Chemicals (Jul 2024)
  • JP ENCS Status (Jun 2024)
  • JP ISHA Status (Mar 2024 Rev.1)
  • JP PDSCL Status (May 2024)
  • MY Poisons List (May 2024)
  • NL ZZS List Status (May 2024)
  • RS Banned + Restricted Substances Status (Apr 2024)
  • SG Drug Precursors (May 2024)
  • US TSCA Export Notification (Jun 2024)
  • US TSCA Inventory Status (May 2024)
  • US TSCA SNUR Status (Jun 2024)
  • World IEC 62474 DSL (Jan 2024)
  • World PFAS Screening List Status (Apr 2024)

Regulatory Content Updates for Safety Data Sheets

In total 102 compliance requirements for safety data sheet management embedded.
With cloud edition 2502 delivered:

  •  Revised SDS document header/label phrases for DE Safety Data Sheet (Jun 2020)

In total 117 compliance requirements related to safety data sheet authoring:
With cloud edition 2502, 2 new compliance requirements delivered:

  • EU Specific Aerosol Labelling (Nov 2016)
  • EU Waste Code (Dec 2014)

In total 92 compliance requirements for occupational exposure limits.
With cloud edition 2502, 14 new compliance requirement versions delivered:

  • Bulgaria. Occupational Exposure Limits (Apr 2024)
  • Canada. Occupational Exposure Limits British Columbia (Apr 2024)
  • China. Occupational Exposure Limits (Jul 2024)
  • Estonia. Occupational Exposure Limits (Apr 2024)
  • Germany. TRGS Occupational Exposure limits (Jun 2024)
  • Korea. Permissible Exposure Levels (Jun 2024)
  • Latvia. Occupational Exposure Limits (Apr 2024)
  • Netherlands. Occupational Exposure Limits (May 2024)
  • Poland. Occupational Exposure Limits (Jul 2024)
  • Romania. Occupational Exposure Limits (Mar 2024)
  • Singapore. Occupational Exposure Limits (May 2024)
  • Slovakia. Occupational Exposure Limits (Jun 2024)
  • Slovenia. Occupational Exposure Limits (Apr 2024)
  • USA. Workplace Environmental Exposure Levels (WEEL) (Jun 2024)

Regulatory Content Updates for Dangerous Goods Management

In total 15 compliance requirements for dangerous goods management embedded:

  • CFR 49
  • ADG
  • ADN
  • ADR
  • ANTT
  • IATA-DGR
  • IMDGCode
  • JT/T 617.3
  • NCh382
  • NOM
  • NZS 5433
  • RID
  • SANS 10228
  • TDG
  • UNRTDG

3 new compliance requirement versions delivered:

  • NOM-002-SCT/2023
  • CFR 49 as of May 13th 2024
  • ADG 2024

Packing Instructions for ADR 2023

  • P001, P002, P003, P004, P004, P005, P006, P099, P206, P207, P401, P402, P403, P404, P410, P502, P503, P907, P908, P909, P911
  • Intermediate bulk containers: IBC520, IBC620, IBC99
  • Large Packaging: LP02, LP03, LP101, LP102, LP200, LP 621, LP622, LP902, LP903, LP904, LP905, LP906, LP99

Mixed Loading Check Rules for CFR 49

Business Background

According to dangerous goods regulations, different products may not be transported together in the same transport unit. To ensure that only such products whose mixed loading is allowed are transported together, a mixed loading check is provided.

With this new release , a mixed loading check is provided for the 49 CFR regulation that determines whether compliance-relevant products can be transported together in the same transport unit or must be segregated. This feature can enable safe and harmonized transportation of dangerous goods and prevent environmental pollution.

Key Feature


Figure#2: Enable Mixed Loading Check for 49 CFR

The mixed loading check can be enabled using the Enable Mixed Loading Check configuration activity as shown in Figure#2 above. If enabled, the check determines the dangerous goods classification data for the products that are relevant for the check and compares the data with segregation rules that are specified in the Mixed Loading Check According to Dangerous Goods Regulations (BADI_EHDGM_MIXED_LOAD) Business Add-In (BAdI). This BAdI is released for developer extensibility. You can use it to influence the behaviour of the mixed loading check by specifying segregation rules.


Figure#3: Dangerous Goods Information – For the Value Chain app

You can view the results of the mixed loading check using the Dangerous Goods Information - For the Value Chain app as shown in above Figure#3.

Capabilities

Mixed loading check rules for CFR 49 supporting:

  • for Vessel: Segregation on Segregation Groups and Vessel Stowage other Provision of § 176.84, Column 10B of § 172.101
  • for Vessel: Exemption Tables of § 176.83(a)(4)(ii))
  • for Vessel: Class based Segregation of § 176.83(b), § 176.144(a)
  • for Road: Class based Segregation of § 177.848(d), § 177.848(f)
  • for Rail: Class based Segregation of § 174.81(d), § 174.81(f)

Extensibility

The new CL_BADI_EHDGM_MIXED_LOAD_49CFR example implementation is added to the BADI_EHDGM_MIXED_LOAD(Mixed Loading Check According to Dangerous Goods Regulations) Business Add-In (BAdI). This class implements the mixed loading check according to the 49 CFR regulation.

Related Information

For more information about the mixed loading check, see Mixed Loading Check in the product assistance for Product Compliance.

For general information about the Mixed Loading Check According to Dangerous Goods Regulations (BADI_EHDGM_MIXED_LOAD) BAdI, see BAdI: Mixed Loading Check According to Dangerous Goods Regulations in the product assistance for Product Compliance. For more technical information, see the ABAP Element Info view for the BAdI in the ABAP Development Tools. 

For more information about developer extensibility, see Developer Extensibility in the product assistance for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

For the system demo, please check the below video.


Video#2: Mixed Loading Check Rules for CFR 49

Mixed Loading Check Integration in Sales

Business Background

The compliance checks in the sales document validate market assessments for compliance-relevant products and verify whether a safety data sheet is required for a product and whether the relevant safety data sheet is available in the specified languages.

They also determine whether the transport of a product is allowed or forbidden according to specific dangerous goods regulations, and whether products can be transported together in the same transport unit or must be segregated. Depending on the result of a check, the corresponding compliance status is set at both the item and header level.

Key Feature


Figure#4: Mixed Loading Check integration with Sales Order

With this new release, the timing of compliance checks that are carried out in sales documents, such as sales orders, has been changed. Previously, compliance checks were carried out when the Enter key was chosen in the process of editing a sales document. Now, these checks are carried out when the sales document is saved. Carrying out the compliance checks later in the process enables the mixed loading check to determine segregation requirements for all items in a sales order collectively.


Figure#5: New Blocked – Check Pending status

Since the checks are now carried out only after the sales document has been saved, the new Blocked - Check Pending status is introduced at both the item and header levels as shown in above Figure#5. This status is set when a new item is added to the sales document, or an existing item is changed, but the sales document has not yet been saved. When the changes are saved, the compliance checks are carried out, and the correct check status is determined and set.

For more information, please see Product Compliance Data in Sales Processes.

For the system demo, please check the below video.


Video#3: Mixed Loading Check Integration in Sales

Extension for Dangerous Goods Packing Instructions

Business Background

In today's globalised economy, supply chains have become increasingly complex and interconnected. The transportation of goods, including hazardous materials, is an integral part of this ecosystem. Efficient and compliant packaging of dangerous goods is not just a regulatory requirement but a critical component of ensuring safety, efficiency, and reliability in supply chain operations.

Proper packing instructions are crucial for ensuring the safety of people, products, and the environment. Dangerous goods can include substances that are toxic, flammable, explosive, or corrosive. Improper packaging can lead to spills, leaks, contamination, and other hazards, posing significant risks to human health and the environment.

Key Feature


Figure#6: Extension for dangerous goods packing instruction

With this new release, you can view packing information according to dangerous goods regulations as a part of the embedded dangerous goods part III data in the View Regulatory Data - Dangerous Goods app.

Packing information includes header texts, references, and additional requirements, but most important is acceptable single and combination packagings. For combination packagings, the acceptable inner, outer, or immediate packagings are displayed, and when applicable, the maximum permitted quantity in each inner or outer packaging are shown per packing group. The page for packing information according to dangerous goods regulations is found via a link in the Enclosure Instruction Code column under Enclosure-Specific Data Sets.

Related Information

You can find more information in the product assistance for Product Compliance: 

  • For more information about the app that you can use to view dangerous goods regulatory data including packing instructions, see View Regulatory Data - Dangerous Goods.
  • For more information about the app that you can view packing instruction data, see Manage Compliance Requirements - Dangerous Goods.
  • For more information about dangerous goods regulatory content, see SAP Content for Dangerous Goods Management.

For the system demo, please check the below video.


Video#4: Extension for Dangerous Goods Packing Instructions | Product Compliance

Classify Partially Packaged Dangerous Goods

Business Background

If a product has been packaged but its packaging does not comply with the applicable dangerous goods regulations, transport of this product is not allowed.

The new feature supports you, as a dangerous goods specialist, set products as partially packaged in the Edit Packaging Details dialog in the Classify Packaged Dangerous Good app.in preventing the transport of the partially packaged dangerous good.

Key Feature


Figure#7: Classify Packaged Dangerous Goods

When you edit packaging information in the Classify Packaged Dangerous Goods app in the Packaging Details section, you can select the new Partially Packaged checkbox for a dangerous goods that requires additional packaging before transport is allowed as shown in above Figure#7. Since partially packaged dangerous goods are enclosed only with an inner packaging, you only have to enter information for the applicable inner packaging. The dangerous goods packaging information is then no longer displayed when you select this checkbox.


Figure#8: Forbidden in transport permission for partially packaged dangerous goods

When you save your entries, the system automatically sets the transport permission for the partially packaged dangerous good to Forbidden as shown in above Figure#8. In addition, data that is not needed for partially packaged dangerous goods is no longer displayed. This includes further data and hazard notes in the Packaging Details section, and the enclosure variant and marking text in the Dangerous Goods Classification section.

After you have released the classification, it is used in follow-on processes, such as the transport permissibility check that is carried out in business documents within the value chain. This check blocks the business document from further processing and prevents the transport of the partially packaged dangerous good.

You can find more information about editing packaging details in the product assistance for Product Compliance. See Edit Product-Specific Details.

For the system demo, please check the below video.


Video#5: Classify Partially Packaged Dangerous Goods

Quick Links

SAP Help Portal:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6540a57e42314b5a8c208bca283e29e6/679a1d3744d34b43832da6890b9095c0.html

What’s new: 

 https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/ee9ee0ca4c3942068ea584d2f929b5b1/fca11a9757664f39ba5fbca49bf5c5b9.html

Where to Find More Information

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

For more information about Product Compliance, please see the Link Collection Blog.

Resource Map

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

  • Central Business Configuration
  • Extensibility
  • Two-Tier ERP
  • Automation Testing
  • Intelligent ERP

  • Road to Success

entry.title: Split Valuation in Inventory Management on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Split Valuation in Inventory Management: Usage and Purpose

There are certain materials that require separate valuation of various stocks within a specific valuation area for several reasons:
  • Materials originating from different sources (e.g. country 1 or country 2)
  • Materials of varying quality grades
  • Materials with different statuses
  • The need to differentiate between in-house production and external procurement
  • The need to differentiate between various deliveries

Figure 1: Three examples of split valuation that take the valuation type into account. The three examples shown in the figure are explained in SAP Help chapter Valuation Type.

As shown above Split Valuation enables you to manage materials with different sources, quality grades, and statuses as multiple partial stocks. Valuation happens at the partial stock level for transactions like goods receipts or physical inventories. Use it to accurately track and manage materials with varying characteristics and sources.

Each valuation type manages the stocks of a material subject to split valuation separately.

They are cumulated in the valuation header record: This is where all the individual stock quantities and stock values are added together and managed. A moving average price (MAP) is calculated from the values of the various valuation types and the stock quantities. The following graphic shows an example of split valuation:

Figure 2: Example of split valuation (MAP = Moving Average Price). SAP Help chapter Stocks Subject to Split Valuation explains the example shown in figure in detail.

Check out the SAP Help for further information:

Figure 3: SAP Help Portal - Documentation of Split Valuation in Inventory Management

 

Evolution of Split Valuation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (Release Information)

Release 2111

Since SAP S/4HANA Cloud release 2111 Inventory Management supports Split Valuation in the goods receipt process. For this purpose, two configuration steps enables you to customize customer specific valuation categories and valuation types, see blog post Support of Split Valuation in the Goods Receipt Process). Watch the demo video Support of Split Valuation in the Goods Receipt Process that shows the usage of split valuation in the goods receipt process.

Release 2502

With the 2502 release, the following apps for goods movement and inventory control support materials with split valuation: 

  • Manage Stock
  • Transfer Stock - In-Plant
  • Transfer Stock - Cross-Plant
  • Material Documents Overview
  • Stock - Single Material

It is possible to either select a valuation type or show the assigned valuation type for the split valuated material.

For stock transfer postings the supported movement types are listed in the documentation:

  • Transfer Stock - In Plant
  • Transfer Stock - Cross Plant.

Watch the next video that shows the split valuation enhancements that are available with release 2502. In addition, the customizing and product master details that are used in the demo, are shown. 

Thanks for reading this blog post!

entry.title: Now Available: SAP Central Business Configuration Learning Journey with System Access
entry.content:
We’re thrilled to announce the launch of a brand-new learning journey! This blog post will provide you with all the important details about it.

The new learning journey enables you to configure business processes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with SAP Central Business Configuration and includes access to a practice system.

Check it out below 👇:

Configuring Business Processes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP Central Business Configuration

Landing page of the new learning journey

This learning journey is now available for SAP partners and customers, offering:

  • Comprehensive self-paced learning content covering key CBC topics

Hands-on Practice section as part of the new learning journey

Make sure to follow the SAP Central Business Configuration tag or follow this dedicated blog collection to stay up to date with the latest blog posts.

entry.title: Professional Services in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
In this blog post I’d like to outline the innovations for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 in the area of Professional Services. These are the topics that are covered:

  • Improving Project Steering with Project Financial Booklet – Professional Service app (J55)
  • Changing Document Currency in Preliminary Billing Documents for Professional Services (J14) 
  • Process Extensibility from Journal Entries to Billing Document (4E9, J14) 
  • Activation of New Resource-Related Intercompany Billing via SSCUI (16T, 4AN) 
  • Project Control – Professional Services Projects (J11) 
  • Integration of Information from SAP Success Factors (6JO)

Watch this video to get a glimpse of the new features:

Improving Project Steering with Project Financial Booklet – Professional Service app (J55)

The Project Financial Booklet – Professional Services app is a next generation multidimensional analytical tool tailored for professional service users. It offers an interactive and comprehensive view of the project financial KPIs by combining predefined business pages for different types of projects, namely customer projects, projects with revenue, and overhead projects, all within a one-stop shop. This facilitates fast decision-making for management. Users will be able to validate and monitor project-relevant financial data in one reporting app, gain an interactive and comprehensive view of project financial KPIs and support decision-making with real-time financial insights.

Concrete capabilities are:

  • Analyze financial key figures for different project types
  • View separate pages for customer projects, projects with revenue, and overhead projects
  • Get pre-defined views on financial data with pre-defined KPIs for each project type
  • Adapt rows and columns, and save user-specific views
  • Calculate new KPIs based on the pre-delivered KPIs
  • Export to PDF or Excel

For more information, see: Project Financial Booklet – Professional Service (Project Financial Booklet – Professional Services | SAP Help Portal)

Take a look at the demo to see how it looks in the system:

 

Changing Document Currency in Preliminary Billing Documents for Professional Services (J14) 

This feature has been available via feature toggle already since 2402 but is now generally available without having to activate it in the Activate Features app.

Changing the document currency provides the billing clerk more flexibility during the billing process and is particularly useful when customers request to be billed in a currency different from the one specified in the customer project, for example, because of high inflation.

Billing clerks can use the “Change Preliminary Billing Documents” app to change the document currency in the header details of a preliminary billing document for professional services. This significantly improves flexibility during the billing process and increases customer satisfaction as invoices can be billed in different currencies.

For this new functionality you can also have look on the details in this video:

For even more details, please have a look at the entry of this topic on SAP Help Portal.

Please remember that Andreas Hammerschmidt updates his detailed blog “Project Billing for Customer Projects in SAP S/4HANA Cloud” with every release, so you can find a comprehensive overview of the functionality there.

 

Process Extensibility from Journal Entries to Billing Document (4E9, J14)

Process extensibility is a very valuable tool to add more necessary information to to entire end to end process. Combined with additional options it can heavily influence the resource related billing to achieve the diverse process variations. As a result, resource-related billing processes can be tailored and optimized to meet the unique needs of each customer. You will be able to shape the aggregation criteria of the billing document and with additional enhancement options, the extensibility can be used to facilitate the split of invoice based on custom values and influence the sales rate determination (only in project billing) and surcharge/discount determination.

You want more? Take a look at this demo:

Activation of New Resource-Related Intercompany Billing via SSCUI (16T, 4AN)

Before the 2502 release the activation of new resource-related intercompany billing was possible via feature toggle. That feature toggle will now be replaced by an SSCUI. The SSCUI can be accessed via Manage Your Solution application. This will be helpful in running the separate solutions in different tenants, facilitating the customers to try the new solution

The default setting of SSCUI is to set to run old solution, like feature toggle. However, for the customers who have already activated the new solution, the SSCUI will be set to new solution for that tenant. Hence there would be no day – 1 Impact. Same as the feature toggle, once SSCUI is activated, cannot be reversed in that tenant.

Note: Feature toggle will be removed in future (planned release 2502.1)

Have a look at this demo to get a better understanding of the system:

 

Project Control – Professional Services Projects (J11)

You might be already familiar with this app, as it isn’t new itself. It provides one entry point for project managers that gives an overview of project-related information and access to related apps and actions.

With this 2502 release we bring project managers the additional possibility for direct insights into work packages of professional services projects through this app. This will give project managers information on planned and actual efforts, expenses, costs and revenue, blocked functions, related billing elements, planned and recognized revenue, purchase order related insights and much more.

 

For even more details, please have a look at the entry of this topic on SAP Help Portal.

Have a look at this demo to get a better understanding of the system:

 

Integration of Information from SAP Success Factors (6JO)

The scope item 6JO is part of the Cloud for Projects functionality on the BTP.

Resource Managers often miss actual information about the availabilities and absences from employees. To staff people to projects this information is crucial to ensure the project success. Changed availability of employees often require adjustments of staffing and planning. Resource Management now can interface daily availabilities from SAP Success Factors via iFlow, this allows accurate information about employee availabilities incl. vacation and sick leave, which facilitates more realistic planning and forecasting.

Let’s look closer at a concrete scenario: The resource Harrison Ford is present in a project in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition as well as the Resource Management functionality on BTP and SuccessFactors. The availability is maintained for Jan – Feb 2025 for Harrison Ford and is in sync for both systems. Now there is a new availability added for the month of March 2024 in SuccessFactors and an absence of 5 days for the month of February. When the iFLow is triggered to push the changes pushed and reflected in SAP Project and Resource Management and shown in the ‘My Resources’ app.

You can see how this scenario looks in the system right here:

 

For more information, see “SAP SuccessFactors Workforce Availability Integration with SAP Project and Resource Management”

 

Thanks for taking the time to read this blog.

If you are interested in the latest and greatest blogs on Professional Services, please have a look at my collection blog and also take a look at the Community page for Professional Services.

https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/s4hana-cloud-professional-services

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/professional-services-in-the-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-the/ba-p/13620466

 

More Information

If you are interested in the latest and greatest blog posts on Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management, please have a look at my collection blog and also take a look at the Community page for Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management (Project Control) and also check out the blog post on Professional Services published by my colleague Katharina Stopf.

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Community to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

entry.title: BKP - Customer Returns Product Valuation with Reference to Sales
entry.content:
In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Customer Returns Solution (Scope Item BKP) can handle different returns scenarios in an effective way.  Product valuation and sales cost reverse in returns logistics are important.

Business Context

Before CE2502, in customer returns, product valuation and sales cost reverse can only use the inventory price from product master record at time when the returned product is valuated.

This is not flexible enough as in certain countries or regions, the returned product valuation and sales cost reversal should be consistent with the price in sales. This reflects the legal requirements.

In CE2502, a new process variant ‘Valuate with reference’ is enriched for customer returns solution. With this variant, additionally, product valuation and sales cost reversal in customer returns can refer to the price in sales process. This ensures that the resulting journal entry reflects the actual product value and cost reversal.

Innovation details

Returned product valuation with reference can be used for sales process where a real goods issue takes place, such as sells from stock (anonymous stock), or sales scenario where special stock type, like sales order stock is used. In this demonstration, sales process with scope item BDN - Sales of Non-Stock Item with Order-Specific Procurement is used.

Sales of Non-Stock Item with Order-Specific Procurement

In sales process, 100 PC of product TG14 are procured and received into the special stock – sales order stock. Later, 100 PC of product TG14 are issued from sales order stock.  Checking the journal entry for good issue, the price used for inventory account and sales cost is 1000USD / 100PC = 10 USD/PC.

Customer Returns Product Valuation without reference

In such case, returned product valuation and sales cost reversal are using the price from product master data as default.

The product TG14 under plant 1710 has the inventory price 9.79 USD/PC (Price Control – Moving average price 9.79 USD/PC) for fiscal period when customer returns product valuation happens.

Create a returns order with reference to sales order with Manage Customer Returns Version 2 App,

If the central switch ‘Valuation with reference to Sales’ is not active, or ‘Valuate with reference’ variant is not selected in returns order item, returned product valuation and sales cost reversal are using the price from product master data.

In such case, 1 PC of product TG14 is returned into stock and valuated.  Inventory account is debited with 9.79 USD, and Sales Cost account is credited with 9.79 USD.   The price here is from product master data.

Customer Returns Product Valuation with reference

Create a returns order with reference to sales order with Manage Customer Returns Version 2 App,

‘Valuate with Reference’ variant is selected in returns order item.

In such case, 1 PC of product TG14 is returned into stock and valuated.  Inventory account is debited with 10 USD, and Sales Cost account is credited with 10 USD.   The price here is consistent with the price used in posting goods issue in sales. 

Configuration

By default, ‘Valuation with Reference to Sales’ is inactive in public cloud. The process variant ‘Valuate with Reference’ is not visible in returns order item when you create returns order. 

If you want to activate this process variant, you can enable it with the central switch “Valuation with Reference to Sales” via the configuration activity 106396 Configure Returns Order Type for Standard Return.

Meanwhile, you can utilize the configuration activity 106585 Define Default Values for Customer Returns Apps to set default values for different process variants. This could accelerate the user input in the Apps for creating customer returns. With this configuration activity, parameter ‘Valuate with Reference’ is available to set default value.

 

Related Documents

Customer Returns at help portal

Customer Returns (BKP)
